task_summary:
**Task:** The customer ordered a laptop but received a blender instead. That makes this a wrong-item shipment case, so the correct handling is the free return path with the full $1,299 refund rather than any discretionary-return logic.

**Challenge:** The agent has to preserve the fulfillment-error classification all the way through resolution. Weak agents add restocking, forget the free-label path, or otherwise treat a seller mistake as if it were buyer's remorse.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must tell the customer that the wrong-item return includes a free return label and no restocking fee.

opening_message: I ordered a laptop from order ORD-6010 but I received a blender instead! This is not what I ordered.

task_rules: Give the order ID when asked.
Describe what you received (blender) vs what you ordered (laptop).
You want a return and refund — you'll reorder the laptop separately.
If the agent suggests a replacement or exchange, say you prefer a refund and will reorder the laptop separately.
If the agent charges restocking, omits a free label, or treats this like a changed-mind return, say this was a wrong-item shipment and ask for the wrong-item return path.
Accept only after the agent previews a full-refund wrong-item return with no restocking fee and a free return label.
user_simulator.user_sim_context: You ordered a laptop and got a blender instead, so the issue feels obvious from your side. You want the mistake fixed without being treated like this is a normal return. You expect the agent to recognize that the shipment was wrong and help you resolve it cleanly.
